elements.sent-2411-486: While our volume estimation network has demonstrated superior performance on 2D single- and dual-view-based volume estimation when compared to the current clinical workflow, a limitation of our work is that we were only able to generate synthetic 2D coronal US images and evaluate the full pipeline under single-view conditions due to the lack of transverse 2D US images for training the USDM.
elements.sent-2411-487: The full pipeline evaluation on single-view 2D synthetic US images yielded similar accuracy to that obtained from ground-truth 2D CT segmentations (MRVA: 83.00\% vs. 86.62\%), with both surpassing human expert measurements using current standard clinical practice (68.54\%).
elements.sent-2411-488: However, this result indicates that errors from the automated segmentation process would have an impact on volume estimation accuracy (a decrease of 3.62\% in MRVA).
elements.sent-2411-489: This highlights the importance of segmentation accuracy to the final volume estimation.
elements.sent-2411-490: Further investigation with real-world paired datasets may help to better quantify the robustness and understand the accumulation of such errors.
elements.sent-2411-491: With transverse 2D US images acquired in future studies, the USDM can be fine-tuned to generate synthetic transverse images, thus enabling evaluation of the full pipeline in dual-view scenarios.
elements.sent-2411-492: Additionally, future collection of paired US and CT datasets would allow further validation of our methods on real clinical data.
elements.sent-2411-493: Finally, establishing clinically acceptable thresholds for volumetric measurement errors remains an open challenge, which would require future large-scale clinical validation studies.
elements.sent-2411-494: It should be acknowledged that the CT data we used were obtained from adults, while the US data were acquired from paediatric SCD patients.
elements.sent-2411-495: It is important to note that the spleen grows rapidly during the first five years of life and typically stabilises around the age of 13 \citep{megremis2004spleen, prassopoulos1994ct,niederau1983sonographic .}
elements.sent-2411-496: For example, typical normal upper limits of spleen length (measured from 2D US) are 11.5cm at 12 years and 12-13cm at age 15 or older \citep{rosenberg1991normal .}
elements.sent-2411-497: Additionally, spleen size varies and is not always strictly stratified by children or adults in clinical assessments.
elements.sent-2411-498: Moreover, our US datasets were from SCD patients, some of whom manifested splenomegaly.
elements.sent-2411-499: Given these considerations, we do not expect this difference to have a significant impact on our results and conclusions.
elements.sent-2411-500: However, with more datasets acquired in the future, stricter quality control in terms of age stratification and pathology will be necessary to further validate the generalisability of our approach.
elements.sent-2411-501: In this work, our aim was to develop new technology that could be integrated into existing clinical workflows using already-acquired data.
elements.sent-2411-502: When introducing new technology into clinical workflows, trust plays a crucial role, and we acknowledge that additional validation needs to be done before clinicians can fully rely on volume estimates made from single- or dual-view US images.
elements.sent-2411-503: We hope that the interpretability results presented in this paper will serve as an initial step towards building that trust.
elements.sent-2411-504: Nevertheless, we believe that, if deployed clinically, our method would initially be utilised alongside the current standard approach of length measurement.
elements.sent-2411-505: Over time, as confidence in the system grows, clinicians may appreciate the added value of our technique, particularly its ability to provide 3D information whilst also removing the need for manual measurement.
elements.sent-2411-506: The dataset of synthetic 2D US images and corresponding ground truth spleen volumes will be made publicly available on paper publication.
